symptoms.symptom_name: Acute excoriation with pinpoint bleeding and serosanguinous weeping of facial and truncal lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Self-excoriation of inflamed acne lesions produces raw, bleeding, weeping surfaces — the defining feature of excoriated acne.
A breached epidermal barrier over colonised skin is the substrate for secondary infection — mandates active screening and safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pruritus driving a compulsive itch-scratch cycle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus is the driver of the itch-scratch cycle producing the excoriation; controlling it is central to management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Open and closed comedones with inflammatory papules and pustules, seborrhoea, facial and truncal distribution

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Comedones plus inflammatory papulopustules on seborrhoeic facial/truncal skin is the classic morphology of acne vulgaris.
Lesions arising from comedones point to acne rather than primary superficial streptococcal/staphylococcal impetigo.
Honey-coloured crusting is characteristic of impetigo; its absence argues against impetigo as the primary process.
Absence of fever/systemic illness argues against established or systemic soft-tissue infection at this point.
Eczema herpeticum is typically accompanied by systemic upset/fever; being systemically well argues against it.
Monomorphic punched-out erosions are the hallmark of eczema herpeticum; their absence is required to exclude it clinically.

symptoms.symptom_name: No surrounding induration, fluctuance, or lymphangitic streaking on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Induration, fluctuance or lymphangitis would indicate cellulitis/abscess; their absence (on the source in-person exam) supports excluding established deep infection — but this cannot be assessed remotely, so it is safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild tachycardia (heart rate ~102) consistent with agitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia is explained by distress/agitation and is a clinician-measured value not obtainable via chat; non-discriminating.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute behavioural agitation with compulsive skin-picking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The behavioural driver perpetuates the excoriation; central to the management and safety plan rather than to the dermatological diagnosis itself.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intellectual disability — carer-dependent, limited self-care capacity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Does not change the diagnosis but materially affects management (carer-delivered care), monitoring (cannot self-report), and the escalation threshold.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Comedones plus inflammatory papulopustules on seborrhoeic skin, with severe pruritus driving excoriation and bleeding, together constitute excoriated inflammatory acne rather than a primary infective or eczematous process.

Afebrile, systemically well, with no spreading erythema, induration, fluctuance or lymphangitis — a negative screen that keeps baseline acuity low while secondary infection remains the safety-net risk.
